svg.aria-label: The model knows before it fails. A 2D map of a coding model's internal state at a decision point shows three separate regions: going in circles (re-running the same failing command), busy but stuck (different commands that all keep failing), and making progress. Because the failing states are separate and readable, a 1.14 MB monitor can flag a failing run early, and on this small model a penalty on the specific repeated command breaks 100 percent of audited loops; a capable 30B model has no such steerable rut.
svg.text: The model knows before it fails
We read a coding model's internal state at a decision point and map it to 2D. The three things an
agent can be doing land in three separate regions, so “stuck” is a real, readable state. The same split holds from 0.5B to 14B (bottom).
“Stuck” has its own region. A 1.14 MB monitor flags a
failing run from the trajectory, ~13 steps before it ends.
A penalty on the exact repeated command breaks 100% of
audited loops at near-zero disruption. Steering alone: 0%.
Bigger models still read “stuck” (the split holds below),
but no vector steers one out: the failure is competence.
THE SAME SPLIT HOLDS ACROSS THE FAMILY
Re-run the same battery on Qwen2.5-Coder 0.5B to 14B and the loop-vs-churn
signal stays at ceiling (AUC ~1.0, length-controlled) across a 28x size range.
So the “stuck” signal a monitor reads is family-wide; only the looping behaviour
and the one-vector steering lever stay small-model.

Each dot in the map is one matched decision-point context, 72 per behaviour; a loop repeats one command, “busy but stuck” tries different commands that all keep failing. Map shown at the 1.5B's sharpest layer.
